revision.text:
{{Infobox Ägyptische Gottheit |NAME = U6-N35-M12
Merencha
''{{Unicode|Mr-n-ḫ3}}'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[Neues Reich]] }} '''Merencha''' (auch '''Mer-en-cha''') ist in der [[Ägyptische Mythologie|ägyptischen Mythologie]] ein gewundener Wasserlauf, der im Land [[Ta-djeser]] die in der [[Duat]] liegenden Regionen [[Sechet-Iaru]], [[Sechet-hetep]] sowie [[Sechet-tjau]] umschließt. Merencha stellt als eine wahrscheinliche Gleichsetzung des [[Flammensee|Flammen-]] oder [[Messersee]]s den Bereich der [[Reinkarnation|Wiedergeburt]] des [[Sonnengott]]es [[Re (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]] und der Verstorbenen dar. Aus einer Beischrift zu der Textpassage des [[Ägyptisches Totenbuch|Totenbuch]]s, die der Region Sechet-tjau in der [[Spätzeit (Ägypten)|Spätzeit]] den Namen gab, ist zu entnehmen, dass die Bewohner von Sechet-Iaru in der Lage sein können, wie Re am Gottesgeburtsplatz, einen [[Sonnenaufgang|Sonnenauf-]] und Sonnenuntergang zu vollziehen. Nach Befähigung kann der Verstorbene an der täglich irdischen Fahrt des Sonnengottes teilhaben; gleichzeitig kann er sich nach seiner Wiedergeburt im Wasser von Merencha in der gesamten Duat wie auch auf der [[Erde]] frei bewegen. == Literatur == * Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * Bertha Porter, Rosalind L.B. Moss: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings; Bd. 1: The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs''. Griffith Institut, Oxfort 1994. [[Kategorie:Ägyptische Mythologie]]

{{Infobox Egyptian Deity |NAME = N36:N35-M12
Merencha
''Mr-n-ḫ3'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国}} }} '''Merencha''' (also written '''Mer-en-cha''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|ja|エジプト神話}}, the name of the winding watercourse that, in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|ja|タ＝デジェル}}, encloses the regions of {{ill|Sechet-Iaru|ja|セシェト・イアル}}, {{ill|Sechet-Hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテプ}}, and {{ill|Sechet-Tjau|ja|セシェト・チャウ}} located in the {{ill|Duat|ja|ドゥアト}}. Merencha represents, as a likely equivalence of the {{ill|Flame Lake|ja|フレームレイク|lt=Flammen-|lt_ja=フラメン-}} or {{ill|Knife Lake|ja|ナイフ湖}}, the realm of {{ill|Reincarnation|ja|転生}} of the {{ill|Sun God|ja|太陽神}} {{ill|Re (Egyptian Mythology)|ja|レ|lt=Re|lt_ja=レ}} and of the deceased. From an inscription accompanying the text passage of the {{ill|Egyptian Book of the Dead|ja|エジプト死者の書}}, which gave its name to the region of Sechet-Tjau in the {{ill|Late Period (Egypt)|ja|エジプト後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期}}, it can be inferred that the inhabitants of Sechet-Iaru are capable, like Re at the birthplace of the gods, of performing a {{ill|Sunrise|ja|日の出|lt=Sunrise|lt_ja=日の出}} and sunset. After qualification, the deceased can partake in the daily earthly journey of the Sun God; simultaneously, after his reincarnation, he can move freely in the water of Merencha throughout the Duat as well as on {{ill|Earth|ja|地球}}. == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター}}, Ethel W. Burney, {{ill|Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings. Vol. 1, The Theban Necropolis. Part 1, Private Tombs.'' {{Infobox Egyptian Deity |NAME = N36:N35-M12
Merencha
''Mr-n-ḫ3'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国}} }} '''Merencha''' (also written '''Mer-en-cha''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|ja|エジプト神話}}, the name of the winding watercourse that, in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|ja|タ＝デジェル}}, encloses the regions of {{ill|Sechet-Iaru|ja|セシェト・イアル}}, {{ill|Sechet-Hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテプ}}, and {{ill|Sechet-Tjau|ja|セシェト・チャウ}} located in the {{ill|Duat|ja|ドゥアト}}. Merencha represents, as a likely equivalence of the {{ill|Flame Lake|ja|フレームレイク|lt=Flammen-|lt_ja=フラメン-}} or {{ill|Knife Lake|ja|ナイフ湖}}, the realm of {{ill|Reincarnation|ja|転生}} of the {{ill|Sun God|ja|太陽神}} {{ill|Re (Egyptian Mythology)|ja|レ|lt=Re|lt_ja=レ}} and of the deceased. From an inscription accompanying the text passage of the {{ill|Egyptian Book of the Dead|ja|エジプト死者の書}}, which gave its name to the region of Sechet-Tjau in the {{ill|Late Period (Egypt)|ja|エジプト後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期}}, it can be inferred that the inhabitants of Sechet-Iaru are capable, like Re at the birthplace of the gods, of performing a {{ill|Sunrise|ja|日の出|lt=Sunrise|lt_ja=日の出}} and sunset. After qualification, the deceased can partake in the daily earthly journey of the Sun God; simultaneously, after his reincarnation, he can move freely in the water of Merencha throughout the Duat as well as on {{ill|Earth|ja|地球}}. == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' {{Infobox Egyptian Deity |NAME = N36:N35-M12
Merencha
''Mr-n-ḫ3'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国}} }} '''Merencha''' (also written '''Mer-en-cha''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|ja|エジプト神話}}, the name of the winding watercourse that, in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|ja|タ＝デジェル}}, encloses the regions of {{ill|Aaru|ja|セシェト・イアル|de|Sechet-iaru|lt=Sechet-iaru}}, {{ill|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテプ|de}}, and {{ill|Sechet-tjau|ja|セシェト・チャウ|de}} located in the {{ill|Duat|ja|ドゥアト}}. Merencha represents, as a likely equivalence of the {{ill|Lake of fire|ja|フレームレイク|lt=Flame|lt_ja=フラメン-|de|Flammensee|es|Lago de fuego}} or {{ill|Knife Lake|ja|ナイフ湖|de|Messersee}}, the realm of {{ill|Reincarnation|ja|転生}} of the {{ill|Sun God|ja|太陽神}} {{ill|Ra|ja|レ|lt=Ra|lt_ja=レ}} and of the deceased. From an inscription accompanying the text passage of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|ja|エジプト死者の書}}, which gave its name to the region of Sechet-Tjau in the {{ill|Late Period (Egypt)|ja|エジプト後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期}}, it can be inferred that the inhabitants of Sechet-Iaru are capable, like Re at the birthplace of the gods, of performing a {{ill|Sunrise|ja|日の出|lt=Sunrise|lt_ja=日の出}} and sunset. After qualification, the deceased can partake in the daily earthly journey of the Sun God; simultaneously, after his reincarnation, he can move freely in the water of Merencha throughout the Duat as well as on {{ill|Earth|ja|地球}}. == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' From an inscription accompanying the text passage of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|ja|エジプト死者の書}}, which gave its name to the region of Sechet-Tjau in the {{ill|Late Period (Egypt)|ja|エジプト後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期}}, it can be inferred that the inhabitants of Sechet-Iaru are capable, like Re at the birthplace of the gods, of performing a {{ill|Sunrise|ja|日の出|lt=Sunrise|lt_ja=日の出}} and sunset. After qualification, the deceased can partake in the daily earthly journey of the Sun God; simultaneously, after his reincarnation, he can move freely in the water of Merencha throughout the Duat as well as on {{ill|Earth|ja|地球}}. == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター}}, Ethel W. Burney, {{ill|Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings. Vol. 1, The Theban Necropolis. Part 1, Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994, ISBN 0-900416-15-7. {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Merencha]] [[de:Merencha]] [[ja:メレンチャ]]

Merencha
''Mr-n-ḫ3'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Merencha''' (also written '''Mer-en-cha''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|ja|エジプト神話|12=draft|12=draft}}, the name of the winding watercourse that, in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|ja|タ＝デジェル|12=draft|12=draft}}, encloses the regions of {{ill|Aaru|ja|セシェト・イアル|de|Sechet-iaru|lt=Sechet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテプ|de|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sechet-tjau|ja|セシェト・チャウ|de|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the {{ill|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|12=draft|12=draft}}. Merencha represents, as a likely equivalence of the {{ill|Lake of fire|ja|フレームレイク|lt=Flame|lt_ja=フラメン-|de|Flammensee|es|Lago de fuego|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Knife Lake|ja|ナイフ湖|de|Messersee|12=draft|12=draft}}, the realm of {{ill|Reincarnation|ja|転生|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Sun God|ja|太陽神|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ra|ja|レ|lt=Ra|lt_ja=レ|12=draft|12=draft}} and of the deceased. From an inscription accompanying the text passage of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|ja|エジプト死者の書|12=draft|12=draft}}, which gave its name to the region of Sechet-Tjau in the {{ill|Late Period (Egypt)|ja|エジプト後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|12=draft|12=draft}}, it can be inferred that the inhabitants of Sechet-Iaru are capable, like Re at the birthplace of the gods, of performing a {{ill|Sunrise|ja|日の出|lt=Sunrise|lt_ja=日の出|12=draft|12=draft}} and sunset. After qualification, the deceased can partake in the daily earthly journey of the Sun God; simultaneously, after his reincarnation, he can move freely in the water of Merencha throughout the Duat as well as on {{ill|Earth|ja|地球|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ethel W. Burney, {{ill|Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings. Vol. 1, The Theban Necropolis. Part 1, Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994, ISBN 0-900416-15-7. {{draft categories|}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Translated Pages]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Merencha]] [[de:Merencha]] [[ja:メレンチャ]]

{{Infobox deity |NAME = N36:N35-M12
Merencha
''Mr-n-ḫ3'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|12=draft|12=draft}} }} '''Merencha''' (also written '''Mer-en-cha''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|ja|エジプト神話|12=draft|12=draft}}, the name of the winding watercourse that, in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|ja|タ＝デジェル|12=draft|12=draft}}, encloses the regions of {{ill|Aaru|ja|セシェト・イアル|de|Sechet-iaru|lt=Sechet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテプ|de|12=draft|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Sechet-tjau|ja|セシェト・チャウ|de|12=draft|12=draft}} located in the {{ill|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|12=draft|12=draft}}. Merencha represents, as a likely equivalence of the {{ill|Lake of fire|ja|フレームレイク|lt=Flame|lt_ja=フラメン-|de|Flammensee|es|Lago de fuego|12=draft|12=draft}} or {{ill|Knife Lake|ja|ナイフ湖|de|Messersee|12=draft|12=draft}}, the realm of {{ill|Reincarnation|ja|転生|12=draft|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Sun God|ja|太陽神|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ra|ja|レ|lt=Ra|lt_ja=レ|12=draft|12=draft}} and of the deceased. From an inscription accompanying the text passage of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|ja|エジプト死者の書|12=draft|12=draft}}, which gave its name to the region of Sechet-Tjau in the {{ill|Late Period (Egypt)|ja|エジプト後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|12=draft|12=draft}}, it can be inferred that the inhabitants of Sechet-Iaru are capable, like Re at the birthplace of the gods, of performing a {{ill|Sunrise|ja|日の出|lt=Sunrise|lt_ja=日の出|12=draft|12=draft}} and sunset. After qualification, the deceased can partake in the daily earthly journey of the Sun God; simultaneously, after his reincarnation, he can move freely in the water of Merencha throughout the Duat as well as on {{ill|Earth|ja|地球|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ethel W. Burney, {{ill|Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings. Vol. 1, The Theban Necropolis. Part 1, Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994, ISBN 0-900416-15-7. {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Merencha]] [[de:Merencha]] [[ja:メレンチャ]]

Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター}}, Ethel W. Burney, {{ill|Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings. Vol. 1, The Theban Necropolis. Part 1, Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994, ISBN 0-900416-15-7. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Pages without German translation box]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Merencha]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Merencha]] [[de:Merencha]] [[ja:メレンチャ]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Merencha]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox deity |NAME = N36:N35-M12
Merencha
''Mr-n-ḫ3'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=}} }} '''Merencha''' (also written '''Mer-en-cha''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian Mythology|ja|エジプト神話|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian Mythology|qq=}}, the name of the winding watercourse that, in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|ja|タ＝デジェル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|qq=}}, encloses the regions of [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]], {{ill|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテプ|de|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sechet-tjau|ja|セシェト・チャウ|de|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|qq=}} located in the {{ill|Duat|ja|ドゥアト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Duat|qq=}}. Merencha represents, as a likely equivalence of the {{ill|Lake of fire|ja|フレームレイク|lt=Flame|lt_ja=フラメン-|de|Flammensee|es|Lago de fuego|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake of fire|qq=}} or {{ill|Knife Lake|ja|ナイフ湖|de|Messersee|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Knife Lake|qq=}}, the realm of {{ill|Reincarnation|ja|転生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reincarnation|qq=}} of the {{ill|Sun God|ja|太陽神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun God|qq=}} {{ill|Ra|ja|レ|lt=Ra|lt_ja=レ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|qq=}} and of the deceased. From an inscription accompanying the text passage of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|ja|エジプト死者の書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Dead|qq=}}, which gave its name to the region of Sechet-Tjau in the {{ill|Late Period (Egypt)|ja|エジプト後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period (Egypt)|qq=}}, it can be inferred that the inhabitants of Sechet-Iaru are capable, like Re at the birthplace of the gods, of performing a {{ill|Sunrise|ja|日の出|lt=Sunrise|lt_ja=日の出|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunrise|qq=}} and sunset. After qualification, the deceased can partake in the daily earthly journey of the Sun God; simultaneously, after his reincarnation, he can move freely in the water of Merencha throughout the Duat as well as on {{ill|Earth|ja|地球|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earth|qq=}}. == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bertha Porter|qq=}}, Ethel W. Burney, {{ill|Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind L.B. Moss|qq=}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings. Vol. 1, The Theban Necropolis. Part 1, Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994, ISBN 0-900416-15-7. {{translated page|de|Merencha|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Merencha]] [[de:Merencha]] [[ja:メレンチャ]] [[sn: failed]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox deity |NAME = N36:N35-M12
Merencha
''Mr-n-ḫ3'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|qq=}} }} '''Merencha''' (also written '''Mer-en-cha''') is, in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian Mythology]], the name of the winding watercourse that, in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|ja|タ＝デジェル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|qq=}}, encloses the regions of [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]], {{ill|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテプ|de|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|qq=}}, and {{ill|Sechet-tjau|ja|セシェト・チャウ|de|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|qq=}} located in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. Merencha represents, as a likely equivalence of the {{ill|Lake of fire|ja|フレームレイク|lt=Flame|lt_ja=フラメン-|de|Flammensee|es|Lago de fuego|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake of fire|qq=}} or {{ill|Knife Lake|ja|ナイフ湖|de|Messersee|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Knife Lake|qq=}}, the realm of [[:en:Reincarnation|Reincarnation]] of the [[:en:Solar deity|Sun God]] {{ill|Ra|ja|レ|lt=Ra|lt_ja=レ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|qq=}} and of the deceased. From an inscription accompanying the text passage of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|ja|エジプト死者の書|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Dead|qq=}}, which gave its name to the region of Sechet-Tjau in the {{ill|Late Period (Egypt)|ja|エジプト後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period (Egypt)|qq=}}, it can be inferred that the inhabitants of Sechet-Iaru are capable, like Re at the birthplace of the gods, of performing a [[:en:Sunrise|Sunrise]] and sunset. After qualification, the deceased can partake in the daily earthly journey of the Sun God; simultaneously, after his reincarnation, he can move freely in the water of Merencha throughout the Duat as well as on [[:en:Earth|Earth]]. == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bertha Porter|qq=}}, Ethel W. Burney, {{ill|Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind L.B. Moss|qq=}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings. Vol. 1, The Theban Necropolis. Part 1, Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994, ISBN 0-900416-15-7. {{translated page|de|Merencha|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Translated from German with ambiguous and strange interlanguage links]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:Merencha]] [[de:Merencha]] [[ja:メレンチャ]] [[sn: failed]]

After qualification, the deceased can partake in the daily earthly journey of the Sun God; simultaneously, after his reincarnation, he can move freely in the water of Merencha throughout the Duat as well as on [[:en:Earth|Earth]]. == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bertha Porter|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Ethel W. ''Mr-n-ḫ3'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Merencha''' (also written '''Mer-en-cha''') is, in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian Mythology]], the name of the winding watercourse that, in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|ja|タ＝デジェル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, encloses the regions of [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]], {{ill|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテプ|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sechet-tjau|ja|セシェト・チャウ|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. Merencha represents, as a likely equivalence of the {{ill|Lake of fire|ja|フレームレイク|lt=Flame|lt_ja=フラメン-|de|Flammensee|es|Lago de fuego|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake of fire|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Knife Lake|ja|ナイフ湖|de|Messersee|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Knife Lake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the realm of [[:en:Reincarnation|Reincarnation]] of the [[:en:Solar deity|Sun God]] {{ill|Ra|ja|レ|lt=Ra|lt_ja=レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|1=レ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9301108}} and of the deceased. From an inscription accompanying the text passage of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|ja|エジプト死者の書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which gave its name to the region of Sechet-Tjau in the {{ill|Late Period (Egypt)|ja|エジプト後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it can be inferred that the inhabitants of Sechet-Iaru are capable, like Re at the birthplace of the gods, of performing a [[:en:Sunrise|Sunrise]] and sunset. After qualification, the deceased can partake in the daily earthly journey of the Sun God; simultaneously, after his reincarnation, he can move freely in the water of Merencha throughout the Duat as well as on [[:en:Earth|Earth]]. == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bertha Porter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Ethel W. Burney, {{ill|Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings. Vol. 1, The Theban Necropolis. Part 1, Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994, ISBN 0-900416-15-7. {{translated page|de|Merencha|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Merencha]] [[ja:メレンチャ]] {{wikidata link|Q1571119}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994, ISBN 0-900416-15-7. {{translated page|de|Merencha|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Merencha]] [[ja:メレンチャ]] {{wikidata link|Q1571119}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q1571119}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[de:Merencha]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1571119]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox deity |NAME = N36:N35-M12
Merencha
''Mr-n-ḫ3'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Merencha''' (also written '''Mer-en-cha''') is, in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian Mythology]], the name of the winding watercourse that, in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|ja|タ＝デジェル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, encloses the regions of [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]], {{ill|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテプ|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sechet-tjau|ja|セシェト・チャウ|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. Merencha represents, as a likely equivalence of the {{ill|Lake of fire|ja|フレームレイク|lt=Flame|lt_ja=フラメン-|de|Flammensee|es|Lago de fuego|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake of fire|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Knife Lake|ja|ナイフ湖|de|Messersee|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Knife Lake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the realm of [[:en:Reincarnation|Reincarnation]] of the [[:en:Solar deity|Sun God]] {{ill|Ra|ja|レ|lt=Ra|lt_ja=レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|1=レ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9301108}} and of the deceased. From an inscription accompanying the text passage of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|ja|エジプト死者の書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which gave its name to the region of Sechet-Tjau in the {{ill|Late Period (Egypt)|ja|エジプト後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it can be inferred that the inhabitants of Sechet-Iaru are capable, like Re at the birthplace of the gods, of performing a [[:en:Sunrise|Sunrise]] and sunset. After qualification, the deceased can partake in the daily earthly journey of the Sun God; simultaneously, after his reincarnation, he can move freely in the water of Merencha throughout the Duat as well as on [[:en:Earth|Earth]]. == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bertha Porter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Ethel W. Burney, {{ill|Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings. Vol. 1, The Theban Necropolis. Part 1, Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994, ISBN 0-900416-15-7. {{translated page|de|Merencha|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Merencha]] [[ja:メレンチャ]] {{wikidata link|Q1571119}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q1571119}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Merencha represents, as a likely equivalence of the {{ill|Lake of fire|ja|フレームレイク|lt=Flame|lt_ja=フラメン-|de|Flammensee|es|Lago de fuego|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake of fire|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Knife Lake|ja|ナイフ湖|de|Messersee|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Knife Lake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the realm of [[:en:Reincarnation|Reincarnation]] of the [[:en:Solar deity|Sun God]] {{ill|Ra|ja|レ|lt=Ra|lt_ja=レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|1=レ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9301108}} and of the deceased. From an inscription accompanying the text passage of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|ja|エジプト死者の書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which gave its name to the region of Sechet-Tjau in the {{ill|Late Period (Egypt)|ja|エジプト後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it can be inferred that the inhabitants of Sechet-Iaru are capable, like Re at the birthplace of the gods, of performing a [[:en:Sunrise|Sunrise]] and sunset. After qualification, the deceased can partake in the daily earthly journey of the Sun God; simultaneously, after his reincarnation, he can move freely in the water of Merencha throughout the Duat as well as on [[:en:Earth|Earth]]. == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bertha Porter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Ethel W. Burney, {{ill|Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings. Vol. 1, The Theban Necropolis. Part 1, Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994, ISBN 0-900416-15-7. {{translated page|de|Merencha|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[de:Merencha]] [[ja:メレンチャ]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q1571119}}

''Mr-n-ḫ3'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Merencha''' (also written '''Mer-en-cha''') is, in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian Mythology]], the name of the winding watercourse that, in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|ja|タ＝デジェル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, encloses the regions of [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]], {{ill|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテプ|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sechet-tjau|ja|セシェト・チャウ|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located in the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. Merencha represents, as a likely equivalence of the {{ill|Lake of fire|ja|フレームレイク|lt=Flame|lt_ja=フラメン-|de|Flammensee|es|Lago de fuego|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake of fire|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Knife Lake|ja|ナイフ湖|de|Messersee|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Knife Lake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the realm of [[:en:Reincarnation|Reincarnation]] of the [[:en:Solar deity|Sun God]] {{ill|Ra|ja|レ|lt=Ra|lt_ja=レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|1=レ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9301108}} and of the deceased. From an inscription accompanying the text passage of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|ja|エジプト死者の書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which gave its name to the region of Sechet-Tjau in the {{ill|Late Period (Egypt)|ja|エジプト後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it can be inferred that the inhabitants of Sechet-Iaru are capable, like Re at the birthplace of the gods, of performing a [[:en:Sunrise|Sunrise]] and sunset. After qualification, the deceased can partake in the daily earthly journey of the Sun God; simultaneously, after his reincarnation, he can move freely in the water of Merencha throughout the Duat as well as on [[:en:Earth|Earth]]. == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bertha Porter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Ethel W. Burney, {{ill|Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings. Vol. 1, The Theban Necropolis. Part 1, Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994, ISBN 0-900416-15-7. {{translated page|de|Merencha|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| }} [[de:Merencha]] [[ja:メレンチャ]] {{wikidata link|Q1571119}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bertha Porter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Ethel W. Burney, {{ill|Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings. Vol. 1, The Theban Necropolis. Part 1, Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994, ISBN 0-900416-15-7. {{translated page|de|Merencha|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Merencha]] [[ja:メレンチャ]] {{wikidata link|Q1571119}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bertha Porter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Ethel W. Burney, {{ill|Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings. Vol. 1, The Theban Necropolis. Part 1, Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994, ISBN 0-900416-15-7. {{translated page|de|Merencha|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Merencha]] [[ja:メレンチャ]] {{wikidata link|Q1571119}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

[[de:Merencha]] [[de:Merencha]] [[de:Merencha]] {{Infobox deity |NAME = N36:N35-M12
Merencha
''Mr-n-ḫ3'' |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|New Kingdom|ja|新王国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Kingdom|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} }} '''Merencha''' (also written '''Mer-en-cha''') is, in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|en|Egyptian mythology|qid=Q205740|lt=Egyptian Mythology|1=Egyptian mythology}}, the name of the winding watercourse that, in the land of {{ill|Ta-djeser|ja|タ＝デジェル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ta-djeser|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, encloses the regions of {{ill|Aaru|en|Aaru|qid=Q618140|lt=Sechet-iaru|1=Aaru}}, {{ill|Sechet-hetep|ja|セシェト・ヘテプ|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-hetep|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Sechet-tjau|ja|セシェト・チャウ|de|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sechet-tjau|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} located in the {{ill|Duat|en|Duat|qid=Q871389|lt=Duat|1=Duat}}. Merencha represents, as a likely equivalence of the {{ill|Lake of fire|ja|フレームレイク|lt=Flame|lt_ja=フラメン-|de|Flammensee|es|Lago de fuego|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake of fire|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} or {{ill|Knife Lake|ja|ナイフ湖|de|Messersee|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Knife Lake|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the realm of {{ill|Reincarnation|en|Reincarnation|qid=Q128593|lt=Reincarnation|1=Reincarnation}} of the {{ill|Solar deity|en|Solar deity|qid=Q511056|lt=Sun God|1=Solar deity}} {{ill|Ra|ja|レ|lt=Ra|lt_ja=レ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|1=レ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9301108}} and of the deceased. From an inscription accompanying the text passage of the {{ill|Book of the Dead|ja|エジプト死者の書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of the Dead|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which gave its name to the region of Sechet-Tjau in the {{ill|Late Period (Egypt)|ja|エジプト後期|lt=Late Period|lt_ja=後期|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Period (Egypt)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, it can be inferred that the inhabitants of Sechet-Iaru are capable, like Re at the birthplace of the gods, of performing a {{ill|Sunrise|en|Sunrise|qid=Q193294|lt=Sunrise|1=Sunrise}} and sunset. After qualification, the deceased can partake in the daily earthly journey of the Sun God; simultaneously, after his reincarnation, he can move freely in the water of Merencha throughout the Duat as well as on {{ill|Earth|en|Earth|qid=Q2|lt=Earth|1=Earth}}. == Literature == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|ja|ロルフ・クラウス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Astronomical Concepts and Afterlife Beliefs in the Pyramid Texts.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|ja|バーサ・ポーター|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bertha Porter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Ethel W. Burney, {{ill|Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja|ロザリンド・モス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind L.B. Moss|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Topographical Bibliography of Ancient Egyptian Hieroglyphic Texts, Reliefs, and Paintings. Vol. 1, The Theban Necropolis. Part 1, Private Tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994, ISBN 0-900416-15-7. {{translated page|de|Merencha|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} [[de:Merencha]] [[ja:メレンチャ]] {{wikidata link|Q1571119}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]
